phonetic: 藢k蓹蕣藢h忙b瑟藞te瑟蕛(蓹)n
examples: Friendship in its usual sense is not tested by the strain of daily, year-long cohabitation.
While cohabitation has been on the rise, the overall share of young adults either married or living with an unmarried partner has substantially fallen since 1990.
The decline in marriage has been offset by a rise in cohabitation.
Cohabitation can cause legal problems the same way marriage can, particularly if the relationship ends.
Cohabitation is bad enough, in Mr Schrameck's view, and Mr Chirac has made it worse.
